q: Transition elements are the metals that exist in the block between Groups ___ and III.
Transition elements are in Groups 3 to ___ of the Periodic Table.
___ is still important as a structural material and used as a catalyst in the manufacture of ammonia.
Iron is also present in ___.
___ is important as an electronic conductor.
Copper-nickel (cupronickel) alloys are used in ___ due to their excellent resistance to seawater corrosion.
___ is the transition element that is used as jewellery because of its beautiful yellow lustre colour and its inertness to oxygen and water.
___ is a major component in modern jet aircraft and is used in manufacturing artificial joints and dental implants.
The role of ___, an essential component of vitamin $B_{12}$ in living systems, is equally important.
A very tiny trace amount of ___ is detected in apples.
The transition elements are located between Group II of the ___ and Group III of the $p$-block elements in the Periodic Table.
IUPAC defines a transition element (metal) as an element whose atom has partially filled ___ (orbitals).
Under this definition, groups 3 to 12 are the transition elements and the $f$-block lanthanide and actinide series are called the ___.
There are mainly three series of transition elements. The first series $3d$ transition elements run from scandium ($_{21}Sc$) through ___ ($_{30}Zn$).
The second series $4d$ transition elements run from ___ ($_{39}Y$) through cadmium ($_{48}Cd$).
The transition elements are also called the ___ because the $d$ orbitals are filled in these elements.
The first row of the transition elements is in the fourth row (Period ___) of the Periodic Table.
In the $3d$ series transition elements, the nuclear charge increases from ___ to copper.
The decrease in atomic radii occurs because the attraction force between the nucleus and electrons is stronger than the ___ force.
All the transition elements in this series have quite similar ___, which accounts for their ability to blend together to form alloys.
Similarly, the first ___ energies (IE) of scandium to copper increase only slightly as the positive charge on the nucleus (atomic number) increases.
Towards the end of the series, ___ has the larger atomic size and ionisation energy.
In atoms of the transition elements, the ___ orbital is normally filled and the rest of the electrons occupy the $3d$ orbitals.
The filling of the $3d$ orbitals generally obeys ___, with one electron added to each of the five $d$ orbitals before a second electron is added to any one of them.
There are two exceptions to the expected regular filling pattern (half-filled or completely filled orbitals); ___ ($_{24}Cr$) and copper ($_{29}Cu$).
In the case of $_{24}Cr$, each of these orbitals must be filled with each electron to attain a more stable electronic configuration with the ___ $3d$ orbitals.
The essential electronic configuration of $_{29}Cu$ is $[Ar] 4s^{1} 3d^{10}$ because completely filled $3d$ orbitals have ___.
In general, the neutral atoms of most of the transition elements have an essential electronic configuration of ___.
When these metals form ions, the electrons are always removed first from the ___ orbital and then from the $3d$ orbitals.
The ___ element has completely filled $3d$ orbitals.
Copper is considered a transition element because the ___ ion has incomplete $3d$ orbitals.
